topic: Configure Azure RBAC role assignments at different scopes
key_facts:
Prerequisites

 To assign Azure roles, you must have:

 
 Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments/write permissions, such as Role Based Access Control Administrator or User Access Administrator 

 
 Step 1: Identify the needed scope

 When you assign roles, you must specify a scope.

Step 5: (Optional) Add condition

 If you selected a role that supports conditions, a Conditions tab will appear and you have the option to add a condition to your role assignment.

Assign Azure roles using the Azure portal

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Feedback 
 
 
 

 
 

 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Summarize this article for me
 
 
 

 
 

 

 
 
 
 Azure role-based access control (Azure RBAC) is the authorization system you use to manage access to Azure resources.
This article describes how to assign roles using the Azure portal.
In Azure, you can specify a scope at four levels from broad to narrow: management group , subscription, resource group , and resource.

Step 2: Open the Add role assignment page

 Access control (IAM) is the page that you typically use to assign roles to grant access to Azure resources.
It's also known as identity and access management (IAM) and appears in several locations in the Azure portal.
